evals.prompt: I'm starting work on adding dark mode support for the client portal
evals.expected_output: The agent creates or finds an existing issue for dark mode support, sets its Status to In Progress on the Nova Work Board, and assigns it to the current developer.
expectations: The agent ran preflight.sh or equivalent gh commands to discover the project board
The agent searched for existing issues about dark mode before creating a new one
The agent created an issue (via create_issue.sh or gh issue create) if none existed
The issue was added to the Nova Work Board with Status set to In Progress or Backlog
The agent assigned the issue to the current user

evals.prompt: This is done, I spent about 3 hours on it
evals.expected_output: The agent detects the current issue from git context, asks for confirmation of hours, and closes the issue with Hours=3 and Status=Done on the board.
expectations: The agent ran infer_issue_from_context.sh or parsed the branch/commits for an issue number
The agent confirmed the issue identity with the user before closing
The agent ran close_issue.sh or equivalent with --hours 3
The agent set Status to Done on the board
The agent recorded 3 hours in the Hours field

evals.prompt: I'm blocked on the auth integration, waiting for the API team to give us credentials
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the current issue, adds a blocked label or comment, and communicates the blocking reason on the issue.
expectations: The agent identified the current issue from git context
The agent added a 'blocked' label or equivalent status indicator to the issue
The agent added a comment on the issue explaining the blocking reason
The agent did NOT close the issue
